chapter_context.summary: Rebukes the wicked counsel of the leaders at the East Gate who compare the city to a caldron, prophesies the sudden death of Pelatiah, and promises the gathering of the exiled remnant, who will receive a new spirit and a heart of flesh.

outline.text: Rebuking the Wicked Counsel of the Leaders at the East Gate
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking the Wicked Counsel of the Leaders at the East Gate (11:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Pelatiah's Death; Promise of Gathering and a New Heart of Flesh
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Pelatiah's Death; Promise of Gathering and a New Heart of Flesh (11:13-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Departure of the Glory of the Lord to the East Mountain
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Departure of the Glory of the Lord to the East Mountain (11:22-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 11, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
